# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS data volumes for application data (Phase 1)

Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instances.
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Description: EC2 instance type for the application server
    Default: t3.medium
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid EC2 instance type.
  LatestAmiId:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>
    Default: /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2
    Description: Identifier of the Amazon Machine Image (AMI) to use for the instance. (Amazon Linux 2)
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: The ID of the VPC where the EC2 instance will be launched.
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: The ID of the Subnet within the VPC to launch the EC2 instance into.
  EbsDataVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Description: Size of the EBS data volume in GB for application data.
    Default: 50
    MinValue: 10
    MaxValue: 16384

Resources:
  ApplicationSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Enable SSH access to the application instance
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0 # WARNING: This allows SSH from anywhere. Restrict in production.
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-ApplicationSG

  ApplicationEC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - DeviceIndex: "0"
          AssociatePublicIpAddress: "true"
          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
          GroupSet:
            - !GetAtt ApplicationSecurityGroup.GroupId
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda # Root volume for Amazon Linux 2 AMIs
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: 8 # Default root volume size for Amazon Linux 2
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
        - DeviceName: /dev/sdh # Dedicated data volume for application data
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref EbsDataVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3 # General Purpose SSD for balanced performance
            DeleteOnTermination: true # Volume will be deleted with the instance for Phase 1 simplicity
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-ApplicationInstance

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: The ID of the EC2 Instance
    Value: !Ref ApplicationEC2Instance
  PublicIp:
    Description: The Public IP address of the EC2 Instance (if in a public subnet)
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationEC2Instance.PublicIp
  PrivateIp:
    Description: The Private IP address of the EC2 Instance
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationEC2Instance.PrivateIp
  ApplicationSecurityGroupId:
    Description: The ID of the Security Group for the application instance
    Value: !GetAtt ApplicationSecurityGroup.GroupId
